value.text:
You don't have to pretend anything. But when someone is working alongside you towards a common goal, you should usually avoid calling them an idiot even if that's what they are.

Everyone who has held a job learns when to STFU, and the same rules apply when you want to achieve a political goal.

record.text: LRT: I dunno, I feel like "I used copilot but I didn't vibe code" is kinda like "I smoked but I didn't inhale." Like.. you *can* define "vibe coding" as using an LLM without any underlying understanding of code, but I think the common usage of "vibe coding" is just "I used an LLM to generate code."

I use language models to develop Ren'Py, and have since Github copilot came out in beta in 2021. Before that I've used other tools (code completion, etc.) 

I don't vibe code Ren'Py, though - that's not something you do if you want a project you can maintain.

images.alt:
They surf the innards of my skin, disrupting the idyllic floating peace of
the Man II and many more. Amongst the disrupted many emerged what must
seem to themself (if indeed they have a self), a hero. A leader of the pack. A
GalT — John, I shall call them — spins, and bores a hole in the outer shell of
one of the invading IgG. Then, seemingly in cooperation, the Man II packed a
passing sugar into the borehole. The warmth of such a vigorous activity spreads through my skin, inspiring more vim and vigour amongst the Man II and the GalT into yet more action.

value.text:
Wearing a Mona Lisa grin,
they smile in knowing,
that death is but another word
for transformation.
They teach us
to release what cannot remain,
to unclench our fists
from what was never ours to keep,
and to bow before each new day,
revering its feral potential.
#poetry #myth #goddesses #witchcraft

images.alt:
Wearing a Mona Lisa grin,
they smile in knowing,
that death is but another word
for transformation.
They teach us
to release what cannot remain,
to unclench our fists
from what was never ours to keep,
and to bow before each new day,
revering its feral potential.
To shed performance
like a costume outgrown,
and step bare-skinned
into the truth of ourselves.
For our time is shorter
than we dare believe,
and that is not the cruelty
we’ve been sold
but a blessing.
Life's impermanence
is not a sentence to endure,
but an invitation
to love deeply,
to live honestly,
to leave behind the masks,
and to spend our numbered days
striving to be exactly who we are.

Thank you.
